message:
fix(http): stateful streaming decompression, quality validation, and Expect: 100-continue refactoring

Gzip and Deflate decompressors now maintain a persistent decoder across
decompress() calls instead of creating a new decoder per chunk. This
fixes incorrect decompression when compressed data arrives in multiple
chunks, as the previous per-call approach would reset the decompressor
state between chunks, losing the sliding window context needed for
back-references. The finish() method now properly flushes the final
decoder state and appends any remaining output.

Accept-Encoding quality value parsing now rejects malformed q-values:
entries with non-numeric q parameters, values outside [0.0, 1.0], NaN,
and Infinity are filtered out instead of silently defaulting to q=1.0.
Previously, "gzip;q=abc" would be accepted with full priority.

HTTP/1.1 server request reading refactored to extract shutdown checking,
expectation flush polling, and expectation classification into standalone
functions (should_stop_reading, poll_pending_expectation_flush,
poll_request_expectation) for readability. ReadOutcome::Read now carries
a continue_sent flag so the serve loop knows whether a 100-continue
interim response was already sent during the read phase. When the
request has Expect: 100-continue but the body arrived eagerly before the
read-phase could send the interim response, the serve loop now sends it
before invoking the handler, ensuring the client sees the 100 Continue
even on fast networks.

Tests added for chunk-based decompression roundtrip (gzip and deflate),
malformed q-value rejection, and eager-body Expect: 100-continue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
